message.content:
You are doing a careful code review of the Trigger.dev worker tasks in the repo at C:\repos\oracle. Read EVERY file under `apps/workers/src/trigger/` and `apps/workers/src/lib/` (and trigger.config.ts). 

Focus on finding: (1) actual bugs (logic errors, race conditions, unhandled errors, incorrect awaits, off-by-one, wrong variable use, broken idempotency, orphaned jobs), (2) inefficient or improperly written code (N+1 queries, missing batching, sequential awaits that should be parallel, redundant DB reads, memory issues), (3) anywhere AI inference is implemented suboptimally or where prompt/context caching is not exploited — e.g. cache-busting prompt ordering (dynamic content before static content), not reusing cached content across runs, not setting cache breakpoints, regenerating context that could be cached.

For each finding give: file path + line number, severity (high/med/low), what's wrong, and the concrete fix. Be specific and cite code. Do NOT make any edits. Pay special attention to the recently-reworked `teams-live-recall-utterance.ts`. Return a concise but complete findings list grouped by file.

This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. 1	# AGENTS.md — The Oracle Developer Guide
2	
3	Read this first. It is the canonical operating guide for developers and AI coding sessions working in `C:\repos\oracle`.
4	
5	## 1. Project summary
6	
7	The Oracle is an evidence-backed enterprise knowledge graph for POP Creations / Spruce Line. Employees interact with it through chat and document uploads; workers extract operational claims with quote-level evidence; deterministic validators gate promotion into approved claims; synthesis workers maintain traceable Brain sections; admin screens review runs, caches, claims, gaps, contradictions, and taxonomy proposals. The outcome that matters is explainable business knowledge: every important answer or synthesis artifact must be traceable back to messages, document chunks, or approved claims.
8	
9	## 2. Multi-model AI note
10	
11	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
12	
13	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
14	
15	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
16

252	## 10. Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
253	
254	### One employee can have many auth identities
255	
256	Looks like:
257	`employees` still has deprecated auth columns, so `employee_identities` looks redundant.
258	
259	Actually:
260	`employee_identities` is the real auth-link table. The deprecated columns remain only as transition leftovers.
261	
262	Why:
263	One person may log in through Google and Microsoft 365 and still be the same employee.
264	
265	Do not change because:
266	Collapsing back to one auth identity per employee breaks real user linkage and RLS helpers.
267	
268	### All inference must go through `OracleAIClient`
269	
270	Looks like:
271	It would be simpler for routes or workers to call provider SDKs directly.
272	
273	Actually:
274	The provider adapters are the only supported inference boundary. They own prompt shaping, provider-native caching, reasoning translation, and usage normalization.
275	
276	Why:
277	The project depends on provider-specific caching and structured-output behavior that generic wrappers and ad hoc calls hide.
278	
279	Do not change because:
280	Direct SDK calls bypass context-pack logging, cache observability, route fallback, and consistent validation.
281	
282	### Log the actual AI result route, not only the planned route
283	
284	Looks like:
285	The caller already resolved a route before calling `OracleAIClient`, so it can log that route in `model_runs` and `model_run_usage_details`.
286	
287	Actually:
288	`ModelRouter` may dispatch to a fallback route. `OracleTextResult` and `OracleObjectResult` carry `routeId`, `provider`, `modelId`, `fellBackFromRouteId`, and `fallbackReason` after dispatch.
289	
290	Why:
291	Cost/cache dashboards and fallback debugging need the route that actually ran, while still preserving the original route when fallback happened.
292	
293	Do not change because:
294	Logging the pre-dispatch route hides provider fallback and makes cache-hit/cost accounting wrong. New AI callers should use the result metadata when writing usage rows, with the pre-resolved route only as a fallback if metadata is absent.
295	
296	### Chat retrieval is deterministic, not AI-SDK tool calling
297	
298	Looks like:
299	The chat route used to define `search_company_knowledge` and `check_open_gaps` tools, so re-adding `tools` to `providerOptions` might seem like restoring agentic retrieval.
300	
301	Actually:
302	The native provider adapters do not execute Vercel AI SDK tool definitions from `providerOptions`. The chat route now performs retrieval deterministically before the model call and injects recent messages, open gaps, approved claims, and Brain snippets into prompt blocks.
303	
304	Why:
305	All inference goes through raw provider adapters to preserve provider-native cache/usage fields. Passing AI SDK tools through this boundary looked useful but was decorative unless every native adapter learned tool orchestration.
306	
307	Do not change because:
308	Reintroducing AI SDK tools in the chat route can create a false sense that the model is searching live. Add retrieval through `searchWithRetrievalPlan()` and prompt blocks, or implement tool orchestration explicitly inside the native adapter boundary.
309	
310	### OpenRouter is enrichment-only
311	
312	Looks like:
313	`openrouter.ts` and OpenRouter-related code mean inference still runs through OpenRouter.
314	
315	Actually:
316	OpenRouter is only used to enrich the admin-side model catalog with pricing/capability metadata.
317	
318	Why:
319	Inference requires native provider features and exact usage fields; catalog enrichment does not.
320	
321	Do not change because:
322	Reintroducing OpenRouter into the inference path would erase provider-native cache and usage behavior that the rest of the system expects.
323	
324	### Explicit Vertex caches are tracked in Postgres
325	
326	Looks like:
327	The adapter could rely only on in-memory cache handles.
328	
329	Actually:
330	`provider_cached_content` is the cross-process source of truth for explicit Vertex cache lifecycle, and `provider_metadata_json` now also tracks cleanup metadata for temporary GCS-backed cache sources.
331	
332	Why:
333	Workers and web requests run in different processes and time windows. Cache accounting and cleanup have to survive process boundaries.
334	
335	Do not change because:
336	Process-local cache tracking leaks money and makes cache reuse/cleanup invisible.
337	
338	### Qwen chat cache state is persisted separately from run usage
339	
340	Looks like:
341	`provider_response_sessions` duplicates data already in `model_runs`.
342	
343	Actually:
344	`model_runs` is audit history; `provider_response_sessions` stores the latest reusable `previous_response_id` keyed by logical session.
345	
346	Why:
347	Responses API session cache needs a stable conversation handle across requests and processes.
348	
349	Do not change because:
350	Without it, Qwen session cache resets every turn and the savings disappear.
351	
352	### Batch API methods on the adapter contract are optional
353	
354	Looks like:
355	`OracleProviderAdapter.submitBatch` and `retrieveBatch` are marked `?` (optional). Existing adapters (Anthropic, DeepSeek, Qwen) don't implement them. The interface looks half-finished.
356	
357	Actually:
358	The methods are optional on purpose. DeepSeek has no public Batch API. Qwen's batch surface is non-OpenAI-compatible (DECISIONS.md D12 deferred the native DashScope SDK swap). OpenAI, Vertex, and Anthropic implement both methods today; future adapters opt in by implementing both.
359	
360	Why:
361	Forcing every adapter to implement batch would either block adding new providers behind a 50%-discount feature, or paper over it with stub `submitBatch` methods that throw — both worse than the optional pattern. The runtime helper `supportsBatch(adapter)` is the feature-detection contract. See DECISIONS.md D14.
362	
363	Do not change because:
364	Making `submitBatch` / `retrieveBatch` required on the interface would force the DeepSeek and Qwen adapters to throw at construction, which would break the per-provider `tryAdd()` boot in `buildStandardAdapters()` and silently disable both providers.
365	
366	Anthropic batch specifics: each request is a `messages.batches.create` entry with `custom_id` + `params` (same shape as a sync `messages.create`); when `jsonSchema` is provided we attach the forced single-tool input_schema per-request (mirrors `generateObject`). Status maps `processing_status: ended` → `'completed'` and we stream `messages.batches.results(id)` to produce `BatchResultItem`s. Per-item `result.type` → success (`succeeded` — tool_use input or text), or failure (`errored` / `canceled` / `expired`). `providerMetadata` is `{}` — the batch ID alone is sufficient for retrieve.
367	
368	### Two-phase batch worker — submit and drain are independent tasks
369	
370	Looks like:
371	`claim-extraction.ts`, `claim-extraction-batch-submit.ts`, and `claim-extraction-batch-drain.ts` look redundant. The flag `extraction_dispatch_mode` toggles them, but all three are scheduled.
372	
373	Actually:
374	Each task reads `settings.extraction_dispatch_mode` at the top and bails when it doesn't match its dispatch mode. So at any given time only ONE of (sync) or (submit + drain) is active. The drain task is always scheduled because it always needs to poll outstanding `provider_batch_jobs` rows even after the flag flips back to `'sync'` (any in-flight batches should still be drained, not orphaned).
375	
376	Why:
377	The flag is read every cron tick — flipping it doesn't require a redeploy. The drain task running unconditionally ensures no orphaned batches when admin flips back to sync. Per-task short-circuit at the top is the cleanest gate.
378	
379	Do not change because:
380	Removing the always-on drain task would orphan any in-flight Vertex/OpenAI batches if the flag flipped back to sync mid-stream. Removing the gate from the sync task would double-process in batch mode.
381	
382	### Model catalog quality filter runs at BOTH write and read time
383	
384	Looks like:
385	The same filter logic appears in `packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/index.ts` (`refreshModelCatalog`) AND `apps/web/app/api/admin/model-catalog/route.ts` (`passesQualityFilter`). Looks duplicated.
386	
387	Actually:
388	Intentional defense in depth. The write-time filter prevents new junk from landing in the DB. The read-time filter handles the legacy case: rows written BEFORE the write-time filter shipped (or when OpenRouter enrichment was unavailable) sit in the DB with no pricing and no capability flags. Deleting them would break pool selections that still reference deprecated model IDs. Filtering at read time hides them from admins without losing the FK target.
389	
390	Why:
391	The write-time-only filter let junk creep back into the admin UI whenever an old OpenRouter outage produced unenriched rows. The read-time filter is the catch-all.
392	
393	Do not change because:
394	Removing the read-time filter resurrects the original "junky models reappear" bug. Removing the write-time filter floods the DB with no-data rows that build up over time. Keep both.
395	
396	### Design file operations are not product/design workflow
397	
398	Looks like:
399	Design-file naming, server organization, invalid filename characters, Photoshop/Illustrator file bloat, linked assets, packaging, and archive cleanup could live under `creative_design`, `product_development`, or `it_systems` because the design team and design tools are involved.
400	
401	Actually:
402	They live in the dedicated top-level domain `design_file_operations`. That domain is for technical creative-file hygiene: keeping design files valid, lightweight, findable, compatible, packaged, versioned, and safe to share. Product/design workflow remains separate: concept intake, design assignment, proofs, approvals, revisions, production handoff, and product lifecycle state belong in `product_development`, `creative_design`, `licensing_approvals`, or `production_lifecycle`.
403	
404	Why:
405	The same employees participate in both knowledge bases, but the user intent is different. "How should I name/save/store this file?" should not retrieve product approval/status claims. "Where is this product in design approval?" should not retrieve filename/server-folder rules.
406	
407	Do not change because:
408	Collapsing these domains causes retrieval bleed between computer/file-management practices and the business workflow of designs/products moving through the company. Keep `design_file_operations` as its own retrieval target and preserve its negative boundary against product workflow domains.
409	
410	### Operations systems are not generic IT support
411	
412	Looks like:
413	ERP, CRM, PLM, Google Sheets, and integration work could all live under `it_systems`.
414	
415	Actually:
416	Business-system data-flow knowledge lives in `operations_systems`. That domain covers ERP/CRM/PLM workflows, field mapping, source-of-truth rules, validation, and integrations such as moving OrderList, MasterData, and TaskList from Google Sheets into Designflow PLM. Generic account access, password resets, permission troubleshooting, and system administration remain in `it_systems`.
417	
418	Why:
419	The Oracle needs to guide operational integration decisions, not just answer technical support questions. "How do I log into Designflow?" and "Which MasterData fields become Designflow PLM item fields?" should retrieve different evidence.
420	
421	Do not change because:
422	Collapsing `operations_systems` back into generic `it_systems` makes business-process integration knowledge compete with IT support noise and weakens retrieval for ERP/CRM/PLM data migration work.

424	### Ineligible models are SELECTABLE (red checkbox), not disabled
425	
426	Looks like:
427	Models that don't meet a stage's required capabilities still have an active checkbox in the model-pool grid, just colored red.
428	
429	Actually:
430	Admin override is intentional. Sometimes a model is fine for a stage even when one of the canonical capability flags is missing (e.g. OpenRouter hasn't enriched the model yet, but the admin knows it supports tools). Disabling the checkbox would force admins to wait for enrichment or edit settings via SQL.
431	
432	Why:
433	The stage-requirements predicates are a heuristic, not ground truth. Red styling + the missing-caps hover tooltip make the override state obvious; no silent risk of accidental selection.
434	
435	Do not change because:
436	Disabling the checkbox blocks valid admin overrides and forces SQL editing.

438	### Stage `thinking` requirement is on Synthesis, not Extraction
439	
440	Looks like:
441	The Extraction model card has no reasoning-effort row visible, while Synthesis does — even though extraction is the more "thinking-heavy" task by feel.
442	
443	Actually:
444	`thinking` was moved from `STAGE_REQUIREMENTS.extraction` → `STAGE_REQUIREMENTS.synthesis` on 2026-05-28 (`apps/web/lib/stage-requirements.ts`). Extraction benefits more from speed + verbatim quote fidelity; synthesis benefits from extended reasoning when consolidating a large approved-claim corpus into a single Brain section.
445	
446	Why:
447	Earlier requirement set forced extraction into reasoning models that produced slower, more elaborate JSON for no provable accuracy win. Moving `thinking` to synthesis matches actual cost/quality observations.
448	
449	Do not change because:
450	Putting `thinking` back on extraction excludes Gemini Flash and the cheap GPT-4o-mini path from the extraction pool — both proven good for extraction in our wet-tests.
451	
452	### OpenAI model catalog uses a blocklist, not an allowlist
453	
454	Looks like:
455	A new GPT or o-series model is missing from the admin catalog even though it appears in the OpenAI API response.
456	
457	Actually:
458	The source in `packages/ai/src/model-capabilities/sources/openai.ts` uses a blocklist of non-chat categories. A model whose name hits a blocked prefix or substring (`-tts`, `-transcribe`, `-search-api`, etc.) is excluded. Otherwise it passes through automatically.
459	
460	Why:
461	An allowlist required a code change for every new OpenAI model generation. GPT-5.x was invisible until `gpt-5` was manually added to the prefix list. The blocklist lets new chat models appear without a code change; only genuinely non-chat categories are excluded. See `DECISIONS.md` D13.
462	
463	Do not change because:
464	Reverting to an allowlist means future GPT-6/o-series models will be silently missing from the catalog until someone notices and edits the list. Post-enrichment quality filters (no-data models, ≥$15.01/1M input) provide a second layer of junk removal in `index.ts`.
465	
466	### Hand-written SQL migrations are authoritative for constraints, views, and data fixes
467	
468	Looks like:
469	Drizzle-generated SQL should be enough, so editing old generated files might be acceptable.
470	
471	Actually:
472	Generated files cover schema DDL only. Constraints, RLS, views, and data migrations live in `packages/db/migrations/sql/*.sql`.
473	
474	Why:
475	The migration runner applies generated DDL plus hand-written SQL in deterministic order.
476	
477	Do not change because:
478	Editing old generated files breaks replay expectations and production drift recovery.
479	
480	### Claim retrieval has exactly one path, and the two SQL branches must stay in lockstep
481	
482	Looks like:
483	`searchWithRetrievalPlan()` in `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts` contains two near-duplicate SQL queries — a hybrid pgvector+tsvector path and a `_searchFallbackTsvector()` path — and a separate `verify:retrieval-filter-parity` script that seems redundant with typecheck.
484	
485	Actually:
486	The fallback runs only when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is unset (dev, no embeddings). Both branches build their WHERE clauses from the same private helper `buildPlanMetadataFilters()`, and the parity guard statically asserts every filter the helper returns is interpolated into BOTH branches. `searchApprovedClaims()` (a weaker, plan-less retrieval) was deleted on 2026-05-28 — there is now exactly one endorsed retrieval entry point.
487	
488	Why:
489	The recurring regression here was adding a narrowing filter to the hybrid path and forgetting the fallback, making dev-mode retrieval silently weaker (or vice versa). Typecheck can't catch it — the fragments are SQL strings. The guard runs in CI (`pr-check.yml`) and via `pnpm --filter @oracle/ai verify:retrieval-filter-parity`.
490	
491	Do not change because:
492	Adding a second retrieval path (or a filter to only one branch) reintroduces the exact silent-divergence class the guard exists to prevent. New narrowing fields go in `buildPlanMetadataFilters()` and get interpolated into both branches — nowhere else.
493	
494	### Raw-SQL list parameters are bound as JSON strings, not JS arrays
495	
496	Looks like:
497	`buildPlanMetadataFilters()` in `packages/ai/src/retrieval.ts` could use the simpler `= ANY(${arr}::text[])` form instead of `IN (SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(${JSON.stringify(arr)}::jsonb))`.
498	
499	Actually:
500	Two driver pitfalls make the obvious forms fail at runtime (found 2026-06-10, the first day a hinted retrieval ran against an approved+embedded claim): a bare JS array in a drizzle ``sql`` template expands to a placeholder list `($1, $2)`, making `ANY((...)::text[])` a syntax error; and binding the array as one param (`sql.param`) relies on postgres-js serialization of unknown-typed params, which is unreliable. Also, a type modifier such as `vector(1536)` cannot be a bind parameter — `EMBEDDING_DIM` is inlined with `sql.raw`. The static verify guards and typecheck cannot catch any of this; it only fails when the query executes.
501	
502	Do not change because:
503	Reverting to `ANY(${arr}::text[])` reintroduces a runtime-only failure that stays invisible until real data exercises the filter. New list filters in raw SQL should follow the JSON-string + `jsonb_array_elements_text` / `jsonb_to_recordset` pattern.
504	
505	### Microsoft Graph Teams transcripts arrive after the call
506	
507	Looks like:
508	The Oracle could "sit in" a Teams call and react live, or read the live transcript panel a bot can see on screen.
509	
510	Actually:
511	Microsoft Graph exposes **no** live caption/transcript API, and Teams does not pipe spoken words into the meeting text chat. The Graph transcript path is therefore after-the-fact, and only if transcription was turned on. Ad-hoc "Meet Now" calls are reachable **only** through a `communications/adhocCalls/getAllTranscripts` change-notification subscription (beta endpoint; v1.0 rejects it), which is "listen going forward" — a transcript notifies only if the subscription existed before transcription started.
512	
513	Live spoken participation now exists through the optional Recall.ai path: Recall provides the meeting bot and STT stream; The Oracle receives finalized `transcript.data` utterances via `/api/teams/live/recall`, writes them as `messages`, and posts only gated questions back to Teams chat through Recall.
514	
515	Why:
516	Native live spoken awareness would require Microsoft media-bot infrastructure. Recall avoids changing Oracle's infrastructure by externalizing the Teams audio/STT transport while keeping Oracle's durable state in Postgres.
517	
518	Do not change because:
519	Do not confuse the two paths. Graph is post-call evidence/backfill; Recall is live optional participation. Both must still write utterances to `messages` and let candidate-before-claim handle durable knowledge. See `docs/architecture.md` § "Teams transcript ingestion" and § "Teams live participation (Recall.ai)".
520	
521	### The Graph subscription/transcript helper is duplicated on purpose
522	
523	Looks like:
524	`apps/web/lib/microsoft-graph.ts` and `apps/workers/src/lib/graph-transcripts.ts` both implement the app-only token + subscription/transcript calls. Looks like copy-paste that should be a shared package.
525	
526	Actually:
527	`apps/web` (the webhook) and `apps/workers` (the subscription manager + ingestion) are separate runtime processes, and cross-app imports aren't allowed. Both need the same small Graph surface. The web copy (`microsoft-graph.ts`) is the reference; the worker copy is intentionally self-contained.
528	
529	Do not change because:
530	Forcing a shared package for ~150 lines pulls Graph code into a third location and couples the web and worker builds. If they drift, reconcile toward the web copy rather than introducing a shared dependency.
531	
532	### ESLint config imports eslint-config-next natively, not via FlatCompat
533	
534	Looks like:
535	`apps/web/eslint.config.mjs` imports `eslint-config-next/core-web-vitals` and spreads it directly, instead of the `FlatCompat`-based pattern `create-next-app` generates.
536	
537	Actually:
538	With `eslint-config-next` v16 + ESLint 9, the `FlatCompat` path throws `TypeError: Converting circular structure to JSON` (the bundled react config has a circular `configs` object). v16 ships a native flat-config array, so importing it directly is the working path. The old `.eslintrc.json` + `next lint` were removed (Next 16 dropped the `next lint` subcommand).
539	
540	Do not change because:
541	Reverting to `FlatCompat` reintroduces the circular-structure crash; reverting to `next lint` breaks entirely (`next lint` no longer exists in Next 16).
542	
543	### Quote validation is fuzzy for transcripts, strict for documents
544	
545	Looks like:
546	`validateQuote` has an `allowFuzzy` path that accepts a quote when its tokens merely *overlap* the source — contradicting the "deterministic verbatim provenance / no fuzzy match" principle in `docs/oracle/03` and the comment at the top of `quote-validator.ts`.
547	
548	Actually:
549	Spoken Teams transcripts are disfluent and the extraction model paraphrases them, so the polished claim quote never appears verbatim in any utterance — strict matching rejected ~every transcript-derived claim. The fuzzy path (opt-in, enabled only on the message/transcript path in `claim-extraction.ts`; documents stay strict) is a **deterministic** token-overlap check (no LLM grader) and anchors the stored evidence to the **real** utterance text, not the model's paraphrase. See `DECISIONS.md` D-transcript-fuzzy-quote.
550	
551	Why:
552	Without it the entire Teams-transcript feature produces zero promotable claims. Provenance is preserved as "this real utterance supports this claim" rather than "the model copied these exact words."
553	
554	Do not change because:
555	Reverting to strict-only re-breaks transcript extraction. If tightening is wanted, raise `fuzzyMinOverlap` or restrict `allowFuzzy` to transcript-sourced messages — don't remove it.

557	### `raw_transcripts` is hand-written SQL, not in schema.ts
558	
559	Looks like:
560	`raw_transcripts` (the original VTT per call) is missing from `packages/db/src/schema.ts`, and the ingestion worker reads/writes it with raw `sql` instead of Drizzle.
561	
562	Actually:
563	It's defined only in the hand-written `packages/db/migrations/sql/62_raw_transcripts.sql` (idempotent `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`), like the observability views. Keeping it out of `schema.ts` avoids a drizzle-kit drift entry for an ancillary raw-storage table the typed query layer never needs.
564	
565	Why:
566	The VTT is stored so the whole pipeline stays re-runnable from true source after Microsoft expires the transcript (`messages` are a lossy transform). See `DECISIONS.md` D-raw-transcripts.
567	
568	Do not change because:
569	Adding it to `schema.ts` would make drizzle-kit want to generate a migration for an already-applied hand-written table (drift). Leave it as hand-written SQL.

1	// R9 — Brain synthesis worker (refactored through OracleAIClient + diff validator).
2	//
3	// Per docs/oracle/05-ai-retrofit-phase-packet.md Phase R9.
4	//
5	// What changed vs the legacy worker:
6	//   - Model calls go through OracleAIClient (R2) via direct provider adapters
7	//     (Vertex / Anthropic / OpenAI raw SDKs — DECISIONS.md D6 / D9) using
8	//     the curated synthesis route from `settings.default_synthesis_route`
9	//     (R1 setting key).
10	//   - oracle_context_packs + model_run_usage_details rows are written for
11	//     every synthesis call so cost / cache / fallback dashboards work.
12	//   - The validator in `packages/oracle-engines/src/synthesis/diff-validator.ts`
13	//     replaces the inline `validateSynthesisOutput`. It adds the R9 rule:
14	//     "Reject unsupported named people, systems, customers, stages,
15	//      departments, or process rules" — every capitalized proper-noun-shaped
16	//     name in `updatedMarkdown` must be backed by an approved claim summary
17	//     or by a canonical entity in the R3.5 registry.
18	//   - On validation failure: the worker now inserts a brain_section_versions
19	//     row with reviewStatus='rejected' and does NOT update
20	//     brain_sections.currentVersionId. The failed output is preserved for
21	//     admin review without touching the current Brain version.
22	//
23	// What stays the same:
24	//   - Two-step transactional insert (spec 6.7) for new approved versions.
25	//   - sectionClaims membership maintenance for claimsAdded.
26	//   - Gap emission + resolution.
27	//   - Scheduled (Monday 6 AM) + on-demand task variants.

31	import { z } from 'zod';
32	import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';

34	import {
35	  brainSections,
36	  brainSectionVersions,
37	  claims,
38	  claimTopDomains,
39	  entities,
40	  gaps,
41	  jobRuns,
42	  modelRunUsageDetails,
43	  modelRuns,
44	  oracleContextPacks,

49	import {
50	  OracleAIClient,
51	  buildStandardAdapters,
52	  getOracleRoute,
53	  resolveRouteFromSettings,
54	  makeBlock,
55	  type OracleModelRoute,
56	  type OraclePromptPlan,
57	} from '@oracle/ai';
58	import {
59	  mapLegacyDomainToTopDomain,
60	  validateSynthesisDiff,
61	  type SynthesisOutput as PureSynthesisOutput,
62	  type SynthesisValidationResult,
63	} from '@oracle/engines';
64	import { KNOWLEDGE_DOMAINS, type KnowledgeDomain } from '@oracle/shared';
65	
66	/**
67	 * Parse the brain section's `relatedDomains` jsonb column (typed as
68	 * `unknown` because Drizzle doesn't constrain jsonb content) into a
69	 * validated list of legacy KnowledgeDomain values. Per spec 10.1, the
70	 * synthesis read scope is the union of `knowledgeDomain` + `relatedDomains`.
71	 * Anything that isn't a known legacy enum value is dropped silently —
72	 * the column is admin-editable and we'd rather lose a typo than crash.
73	 */
74	function parseRelatedDomains(value: unknown): KnowledgeDomain[] {
75	  if (!Array.isArray(value)) return [];
76	  const allowed = new Set<string>(KNOWLEDGE_DOMAINS);
77	  return value.filter((v): v is KnowledgeDomain => typeof v === 'string' && allowed.has(v));
78	}

80	const FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID = 'anthropic_claude_3_5_sonnet_synthesis_primary';
81	const SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_VERSION = '1.0.0';
82	
83	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
84	// Spec 9.8 structured output schema — unchanged from legacy.
85	// The shape mirrors the SynthesisOutput type in @oracle/engines.
86	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
87	
88	const SynthesisParagraphSchema = z.object({
89	  text: z.string(),
90	  supportingClaimIds: z.array(z.string().uuid()).min(1),
91	});
92	
93	const MaterialChangeSchema = z.object({
94	  type: z.enum(['added_claim', 'removed_claim', 'strengthened_claim', 'weakened_claim']),
95	  claimId: z.string().uuid(),
96	  reason: z.string(),
97	});
98	
99	const NewGapSchema = z.object({
100	  questionToAsk: z.string().min(10),
101	  whyItMatters: z.string().min(10),
102	  priority: z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high', 'urgent']),
103	  targetDepartment: z.string().optional(),
104	});
105	
106	export const SynthesisOutputSchema = z.object({
107	  sectionId: z.string(),
108	  paragraphs: z.array(SynthesisParagraphSchema),
109	  updatedMarkdown: z.string(),
110	  materialChanges: z.array(MaterialChangeSchema),
111	  claimsAdded: z.array(z.string().uuid()),
112	  claimsRemoved: z.array(z.string().uuid()),
113	  claimsStrengthened: z.array(z.string().uuid()),
114	  claimsWeakened: z.array(z.string().uuid()),
115	  newContradictions: z
116	    .array(
117	      z.object({
118	        claimAId: z.string().uuid(),
119	        claimBId: z.string().uuid(),
120	        description: z.string(),
121	        severity: z.enum(['low', 'medium', 'high']),
122	      }),
123	    ),
124	  resolvedContradictions: z.array(z.string().uuid()),
125	  newGaps: z.array(NewGapSchema),
126	  resolvedGaps: z.array(z.string().uuid()),
127	  confidenceChange: z.enum(['increased', 'stable', 'decreased']),
128	  requiresHumanReview: z.boolean(),
129	  changeSummary: z.string(),
130	});

132	export type SynthesisOutput = z.infer<typeof SynthesisOutputSchema>;

135	// Payload schema.

137	
138	const PayloadSchema = z.object({
139	  sectionId: z.string().min(1),
140	  trigger: z.enum(['scheduled', 'admin', 'new_claims']),
141	  minNewClaims: z.number().int().min(0).optional(),

144	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
145	// Shared OracleAIClient (one per worker process)
146	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147	
148	function buildOracleClient(): OracleAIClient {
149	  return new OracleAIClient({
150	    adapters: buildStandardAdapters(),
151	    fallbackOnError: true,
152	  });
153	}

155	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
156	// Prompt builder — unchanged from legacy (same shape, same rules).
157	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
158	
159	function buildSynthesisSystemPrompt(
160	  section: { id: string; title: string; knowledgeDomain: string; category: string },
161	): string {
162	  return `You are the Oracle Brain Synthesis engine for POP Creations / Spruce Line.
163	
164	Your task: synthesize a new version of the brain section "${section.title}" (domain: ${section.knowledgeDomain}, category: ${section.category}).
165	
166	MANDATORY RULES:
167	1. Every paragraph in your output MUST cite at least one approved claim ID from the list below.
168	2. Do not assert any fact that is not directly supported by an approved claim.
169	3. Do not name specific people unless a claim explicitly does — and even then, use their role/department instead when possible.
170	4. Do not include information about customers, licensors, or external systems unless a claim supports it.
171	5. If approved claims conflict with each other, report the contradiction — do not pick a winner.
172	6. New gaps must include a specific, actionable question and a clear explanation of why it matters.
173	7. The section ID in your output must exactly match: ${section.id}
174	
175	TRACEABILITY REQUIREMENT:
176	Each paragraph's supportingClaimIds list must contain real claim IDs from the approved list below.
177	The backend validator will reject this synthesis if:
178	  - any claim ID does not exist or is not approved, OR
179	  - the markdown mentions a named entity (person, system, customer, licensor, department) that is not backed by an approved claim or the canonical entity registry.
180	
181	OUTPUT: Return structured JSON matching the schema exactly. The updatedMarkdown field should be well-formatted Markdown suitable for display to the Lead Architect.`;
182	}
183	
184	function buildSynthesisCorpus(
185	  currentMarkdown: string | null,
186	  approvedClaims: Array<{ id: string; summary: string; claimType: string; impactScore: number; confidenceScore: number }>,
187	): string {
188	  const claimList = approvedClaims
189	    .map(
190	      (c) =>
191	        `  - ID: ${c.id}\n    Type: ${c.claimType}\n    Impact: ${c.impactScore}/10  Confidence: ${c.confidenceScore}/10\n    Claim: ${c.summary}`,
192	    )
193	    .join('\n');
194	
195	  const currentSection = currentMarkdown
196	    ? `CURRENT SECTION MARKDOWN (previous version):\n${currentMarkdown}\n`
197	    : '(This is the first version of this section — no prior content.)\n';
198	
199	  return `${currentSection}
200	
201	APPROVED CLAIMS FOR THIS SECTION (${approvedClaims.length} total):
202	${claimList || '(No approved claims yet — output an empty section with a gap asking for foundational knowledge.)'}`;
203	}
204	
205	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
206	// Main synthesis logic
207	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
208	
209	async function synthesizeSection(
210	  sectionId: string,
211	  trigger: string,
212	  jobRunId: string,
213	): Promise<{
214	  ok: boolean;
215	  versionId?: string;
216	  versionStatus?: 'draft' | 'approved' | 'needs_review' | 'rejected';
217	  requiresReview?: boolean;
218	  validationFailures?: number;
219	}> {
220	  const db = getDirectDb();
221	  const client = buildOracleClient();
222	
223	  // ── 1. Resolve curated synthesis route + load section + claims ───────
224	  const route = await resolveSynthesisRoute(db);
225	
226	  const [section] = await db
227	    .select()
228	    .from(brainSections)
229	    .where(eq(brainSections.id, sectionId))
230	    .limit(1);
231	  if (!section) {
232	    throw new Error(`Brain section "${sectionId}" does not exist. Create it in the admin UI first.`);
233	  }
234	
235	  let currentMarkdown: string | null = null;
236	  if (section.currentVersionId) {
237	    const [currentVersion] = await db
238	      .select({ markdown: brainSectionVersions.markdown })
239	      .from(brainSectionVersions)
240	      .where(eq(brainSectionVersions.id, section.currentVersionId))
241	      .limit(1);
242	    currentMarkdown = currentVersion?.markdown ?? null;
243	  }
244	
245	  const lastVersion = await db
246	    .select({ versionNumber: brainSectionVersions.versionNumber })
247	    .from(brainSectionVersions)
248	    .where(eq(brainSectionVersions.sectionId, sectionId))
249	    .orderBy(desc(brainSectionVersions.versionNumber))
250	    .limit(1);
251	  const nextVersionNumber = (lastVersion[0]?.versionNumber ?? 0) + 1;
252	
253	  // Approved claims via claim_top_domains (R3.5) + sectionClaims joins.
254	  // Spec 10.1: the synthesis read scope is the union of the section's
255	  // `knowledgeDomain` and any entries in its `relatedDomains` jsonb array.
256	  // Each legacy enum value is run through the same mechanical mapping used by
257	  // the 42_* backfill SQL and the R6 worker, so this read path stays in
258	  // lockstep with the write path in promotion-executor.ts (which only writes
259	  // claim_top_domains).
260	  const sectionDomains: KnowledgeDomain[] = [
261	    section.knowledgeDomain,
262	    ...parseRelatedDomains(section.relatedDomains),
263	  ];
264	  const topDomainIds = Array.from(
265	    new Set(sectionDomains.map((d) => mapLegacyDomainToTopDomain(d))),
266	  );
267	  const domainClaims = await db
268	    .select({
269	      id: claims.id,
270	      summary: claims.summary,
271	      claimType: claims.claimType,
272	      impactScore: claims.impactScore,
273	      confidenceScore: claims.confidenceScore,
274	    })
275	    .from(claims)
276	    .innerJoin(claimTopDomains, eq(claimTopDomains.claimId, claims.id))
277	    .where(
278	      and(
279	        eq(claims.status, 'approved'),
280	        inArray(claimTopDomains.topDomainId, topDomainIds),
281	      ),
282	    )
283	    .orderBy(desc(claims.impactScore))
284	    .limit(200);
285	
286	  const sectionSpecificClaims = await db
287	    .select({
288	      id: claims.id,
289	      summary: claims.summary,
290	      claimType: claims.claimType,
291	      impactScore: claims.impactScore,
292	      confidenceScore: claims.confidenceScore,
293	    })
294	    .from(claims)
295	    .innerJoin(sectionClaims, eq(sectionClaims.claimId, claims.id))
296	    .where(
297	      and(
298	        eq(claims.status, 'approved'),
299	        eq(sectionClaims.sectionId, sectionId),
300	      ),
301	    );
302	
303	  const allClaimsMap = new Map<string, (typeof domainClaims)[number]>();
304	  for (const c of [...domainClaims, ...sectionSpecificClaims]) {
305	    allClaimsMap.set(c.id, c);
306	  }
307	  const approvedClaims = Array.from(allClaimsMap.values());
308	  const approvedClaimIds = new Set(approvedClaims.map((c) => c.id));
309	  const approvedClaimSummariesLower = approvedClaims.map((c) => c.summary.toLowerCase());
310	
311	  // Canonical entity names from the R3.5 registry. Used by the unsupported-
312	  // named-entity check in the validator.
313	  const registryRows = await db.select({ canonicalValue: entities.canonicalValue }).from(entities);
314	  const registryEntityCanonicalsLower = new Set(
315	    registryRows.map((r) => r.canonicalValue.toLowerCase()),
316	  );
317	
318	  // ── 2. Compile prompt blocks ─────────────────────────────────────────
319	  const systemPrompt = buildSynthesisSystemPrompt(
320	    {
321	      id: section.id,
322	      title: section.title,
323	      knowledgeDomain: section.knowledgeDomain,
324	      category: section.category,
325	    },
326	  );
327	  const synthesisCorpus = buildSynthesisCorpus(currentMarkdown, approvedClaims);
328	
329	  const blocks = [
330	    makeBlock({
331	      id: 'synthesis-system',
332	      label: 'Synthesis system prompt',
333	      kind: 'stable_system',
334	      content: systemPrompt,
335	      reasonIncluded: `synthesis prompt v${SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_VERSION}`,
336	    }),
337	    makeBlock({
338	      id: 'synthesis-corpus',
339	      label: 'Current section markdown + approved claim corpus',
340	      kind: 'retrieved_context',
341	      content: synthesisCorpus,
342	      reasonIncluded: `${approvedClaims.length} approved claim(s) + current section snapshot`,
343	    }),
344	    makeBlock({
345	      id: 'turn-request',
346	      label: 'Synthesis request',
347	      kind: 'dynamic_input',
348	      content: `Please synthesize brain section: ${section.id}\nTrigger: ${trigger}\nApproved claims available: ${approvedClaims.length}`,
349	      reasonIncluded: `trigger=${trigger}`,
350	    }),
351	  ];
352	
353	  const plan = client.compile({
354	    taskType: 'brain_synthesis',
355	    routeId: route.routeId,
356	    promptVersion: SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_VERSION,
357	    blocks,
358	    observability: { includedClaimIds: approvedClaims.map((c) => c.id) },
359	  });
360	
361	  // ── 3. Stage context pack BEFORE the model call ──────────────────────
362	  const [contextPack] = await db
363	    .insert(oracleContextPacks)
364	    .values(buildContextPackInsert(plan))
365	    .returning({ id: oracleContextPacks.id });
366	  if (!contextPack) throw new Error('[brain-synthesis] failed to insert oracle_context_packs row');
367	
368	  // ── 4. Dispatch through OracleAIClient ───────────────────────────────
369	  const callStartedAt = Date.now();
370	  let modelOutput: SynthesisOutput | null = null;
371	  let modelRunId: string | null = null;
372	  let inputTokens: number | undefined;
373	  let outputTokens: number | undefined;
374	  let cachedInputTokens: number | undefined;
375	  let providerRequestId: string | undefined;
376	  let usageRaw: unknown = null;
377	
378	  try {
379	    // The bridge adapter calls Vercel AI SDK generateObject with
380	    // temperature=0.1 by default — sensible for synthesis. If a future
381	    // phase needs to tune temperature for synthesis, extend
382	    // GenerateObjectArgs with providerOptions parallel to
383	    // GenerateTextArgs (R8's pattern) and pass it here.
384	    const result = await client.runObject<SynthesisOutput>({
385	      taskType: 'brain_synthesis',
386	      routeId: route.routeId,
387	      promptVersion: SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_VERSION,
388	      blocks,
389	      schema: SynthesisOutputSchema,
390	      observability: { includedClaimIds: approvedClaims.map((c) => c.id) },
391	      providerOptions: {
392	        cache: {
393	          preferLongLivedCache: true,
394	          preferExplicitCache: route.provider === 'vertex',
395	          cacheTtlSeconds: 24 * 60 * 60,
396	          expectedReuseCount: 4,
397	          persistProviderCacheRecord: route.provider === 'vertex',
398	          sourceDescription: `brain section ${section.id} synthesis corpus`,
399	          cleanupOwner: 'brain-synthesis-worker',
400	          createdByJobRunId: jobRunId,
401	          latestPlannedReuseStep: 'brain_synthesis',
402	        },
403	      },
404	    });
405	    const latencyMs = Date.now() - callStartedAt;
406	    inputTokens = result.usage.inputTokens;
407	    outputTokens = result.usage.outputTokens;
408	    cachedInputTokens = result.usage.cachedInputTokens;
409	    providerRequestId = result.usage.providerRequestId;
410	    usageRaw = result.usage.rawUsageJson;
411	    const actualRouteId = result.routeId ?? route.routeId;
412	    const actualProvider = result.provider ?? route.provider;
413	    const actualModelId = result.modelId ?? route.modelId;
414	
415	    const [modelRun] = await db
416	      .insert(modelRuns)
417	      .values({
418	        taskType: 'brain-synthesis',
419	        model: actualModelId,
420	        provider: actualProvider,
421	        promptVersion: SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_VERSION,
422	        inputHash: plan.metadata.stablePrefixHash,
423	        inputTokens: inputTokens ?? null,
424	        outputTokens: outputTokens ?? null,
425	        latencyMs,
426	        success: result.validation.ok,
427	      })
428	      .returning({ id: modelRuns.id });
429	    if (!modelRun) throw new Error('[brain-synthesis] failed to insert model_runs row');
430	    modelRunId = modelRun.id;
431	
432	    await db.insert(modelRunUsageDetails).values({
433	      modelRunId: modelRun.id,
434	      contextPackId: contextPack.id,
435	      routeId: actualRouteId,
436	      inputTokens: inputTokens ?? null,
437	      cachedInputTokens: cachedInputTokens ?? null,
438	      outputTokens: outputTokens ?? null,
439	      providerRequestId: providerRequestId ?? null,
440	      rawUsageJson: usageRaw ?? null,
441	      fellBackFromRouteId: result.fellBackFromRouteId ?? null,
442	      fallbackReason: result.fallbackReason ?? null,
443	    });
444	    await db
445	      .update(oracleContextPacks)
446	      .set({ modelRunId: modelRun.id })
447	      .where(eq(oracleContextPacks.id, contextPack.id));
448	
449	    if (!result.validation.ok) {
450	      throw new Error(
451	        '[brain-synthesis] model output failed Zod schema validation: ' +
452	          result.validation.error.message,
453	      );
454	    }
455	    modelOutput = result.object;
456	  } catch (err) {
457	    if (!modelRunId) {
458	      await db.insert(modelRuns).values({
459	        taskType: 'brain-synthesis',
460	        model: route.modelId,
461	        provider: route.provider,
462	        promptVersion: SYNTHESIS_PROMPT_VERSION,
463	        latencyMs: Date.now() - callStartedAt,
464	        success: false,
465	        error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
466	      });
467	    }
468	    throw err;
469	  }
470	
471	  if (!modelOutput) throw new Error('[brain-synthesis] modelOutput is null after dispatch');
472	
473	  // ── 5. R9 validator — claim IDs + unsupported named entities ─────────
474	  const validation: SynthesisValidationResult = validateSynthesisDiff({
475	    output: modelOutput as PureSynthesisOutput,
476	    approvedClaimIds,
477	    approvedClaimSummariesLower,
478	    registryEntityCanonicalsLower,
479	    expectedSectionId: sectionId,
480	  });
481	
482	  // ── 6. Insert brain_section_versions row in BOTH branches ────────────
483	  //    Failure: reviewStatus='rejected', currentVersionId untouched.
484	  //    Success: reviewStatus='needs_review' or 'draft', then update
485	  //             currentVersionId.
486	
487	  if (!validation.ok) {
488	    const failureSummary = validation.failures
489	      .slice(0, 10)
490	      .map((f) => `${f.kind}: ${f.detail}`)
491	      .join('; ');
492	    console.error(`[brain-synthesis] validation failed: ${failureSummary}`);
493	
494	    const [rejectedVersion] = await db
495	      .insert(brainSectionVersions)
496	      .values({
497	        sectionId,
498	        versionNumber: nextVersionNumber,
499	        markdown: modelOutput.updatedMarkdown,
500	        structuredContent: {
501	          paragraphs: modelOutput.paragraphs,
502	          materialChanges: modelOutput.materialChanges,
503	          claimsAdded: modelOutput.claimsAdded,
504	          claimsRemoved: modelOutput.claimsRemoved,
505	          claimsStrengthened: modelOutput.claimsStrengthened,
506	          claimsWeakened: modelOutput.claimsWeakened,
507	          confidenceChange: modelOutput.confidenceChange,
508	          validationFailures: validation.failures,
509	          unsupportedNames: validation.unsupportedNames,
510	        },
511	        changeSummary: `[REJECTED] Synthesis failed validation (${validation.failures.length} failure(s)): ${failureSummary}`,
512	        createdByModelRunId: modelRunId,
513	        reviewStatus: 'rejected',
514	      })
515	      .returning({ id: brainSectionVersions.id });
516	
517	    // currentVersionId intentionally NOT updated.
518	    return {
519	      ok: false,
520	      versionId: rejectedVersion?.id,
521	      versionStatus: 'rejected',
522	      validationFailures: validation.failures.length,
523	      requiresReview: true,
524	    };
525	  }
526	
527	  // Validation passed. Insert version + update currentVersionId.
528	  const [newVersion] = await db
529	    .insert(brainSectionVersions)
530	    .values({
531	      sectionId,
532	      versionNumber: nextVersionNumber,
533	      markdown: modelOutput.updatedMarkdown,
534	      structuredContent: {
535	        paragraphs: modelOutput.paragraphs,
536	        materialChanges: modelOutput.materialChanges,
537	        claimsAdded: modelOutput.claimsAdded,
538	        claimsRemoved: modelOutput.claimsRemoved,
539	        claimsStrengthened: modelOutput.claimsStrengthened,
540	        claimsWeakened: modelOutput.claimsWeakened,
541	        confidenceChange: modelOutput.confidenceChange,
542	      },
543	      changeSummary: modelOutput.changeSummary,
544	      createdByModelRunId: modelRunId,
545	      reviewStatus: modelOutput.requiresHumanReview ? 'needs_review' : 'draft',
546	    })
547	    .returning({ id: brainSectionVersions.id });
548	  if (!newVersion) throw new Error('[brain-synthesis] version insert returned no row');
549	
550	  await db
551	    .update(brainSections)
552	    .set({ currentVersionId: newVersion.id, updatedAt: new Date() })
553	    .where(eq(brainSections.id, sectionId));
554	
555	  // sectionClaims membership for newly-added claims.
556	  for (const claimId of modelOutput.claimsAdded) {
557	    if (approvedClaimIds.has(claimId)) {
558	      await db
559	        .insert(sectionClaims)
560	        .values({ sectionId, claimId })
561	        .onConflictDoNothing();
562	    }
563	  }
564	
565	  // New gaps.
566	  for (const gap of modelOutput.newGaps) {
567	    await db.insert(gaps).values({
568	      gapType: 'synthesis_gap',
569	      sectionId,
570	      relatedClaimIds: modelOutput.claimsAdded,
571	      questionToAsk: gap.questionToAsk,
572	      whyItMatters: gap.whyItMatters,
573	      priority: gap.priority,
574	      targetDepartment: gap.targetDepartment ?? null,
575	      status: 'open',
576	      createdByModelRunId: modelRunId,
577	    });
578	  }
579	
580	  // Resolved gaps.
581	  for (const gapId of modelOutput.resolvedGaps) {
582	    await db
583	      .update(gaps)
584	      .set({ status: 'resolved', resolvedAt: new Date() })
585	      .where(eq(gaps.id, gapId));
586	  }
587	
588	  return {
589	    ok: true,
590	    versionId: newVersion.id,
591	    versionStatus: modelOutput.requiresHumanReview ? 'needs_review' : 'draft',
592	    requiresReview: modelOutput.requiresHumanReview,
593	  };
594	}

1	// Contradiction watcher — spec Part 9.3 + spec 5.1 Rule 1.
2	//
3	// R11.0 refactor: all model calls now go through OracleAIClient with direct
4	// provider adapters (DECISIONS.md D6 + D9). The previous getOpenRouter() +
5	// Vercel-AI-SDK generateObject() path is retired. R3 observability rows
6	// (model_run_usage_details + oracle_context_packs) are written for every
7	// adjudication call, matching the R6 / R7 / R9 workers.
8	//
9	// Two modes:
10	//   1. Per-claim task: triggered after a new claim is approved, compares it
11	//      against existing approved claims via pgvector ANN, then calls a small
12	//      LLM to adjudicate whether a semantic match is actually contradictory.
13	//   2. Sweep cron (every 4 hours): finds recently approved claims that have
14	//      not yet been checked and triggers the per-claim task for each.
15	//
16	// Spec constraints:
17	//   - Most possible contradictions should NOT cause live interjections (spec 5.1 Rule 1).
18	//   - The setting `enable_live_contradiction_interjections` gates live interjection.
19	//   - When a contradiction is found, the default decision is 'queued_gap' or 'admin_review'.
20	//   - Log oracle_interventions row if any action is taken.
21	//   - Every LLM call → model_runs + model_run_usage_details + oracle_context_packs.
22

33	  gaps,

35	  modelRuns,
36	  modelRunUsageDetails,
37	  jobRuns,
38	  oracleContextPacks,
39	  oracleInterventions,
40	  settings,
41	} from '@oracle/db/schema';
42	import {
43	  OracleAIClient,
44	  buildStandardAdapters,
45	  embedText,
46	  getOracleRoute,
47	  resolveRouteFromSettings,
48	  makeBlock,

55	// which handles embedding dimensions internally.

60	
61	// Extraction route is the right home for adjudication too — it's a cheap
62	// structured-output call. Different from R11's live-interjection drafting,
63	// which uses the interview route for human-facing warmth.
64	const FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID = 'vertex_gemini_2_5_flash_extraction_primary';
65	const FALLBACK_INTERVIEW_ROUTE_ID = 'anthropic_claude_haiku_4_5_interview_primary';
66	
67	// Default settings if the rows are missing — match seed defaults.
68	const DEFAULT_ORACLE_COOLDOWN_MINUTES = 10;
69	const DEFAULT_MAX_ORACLE_INTERJECTIONS_PER_HOUR = 3;

71	const LIVE_INTERJECTION_PROMPT_VERSION = 'contradiction-live-1.0.0';
72	
73	const LIVE_INTERJECTION_SYSTEM = `You are the Oracle — an evidence-backed knowledge assistant for POP Creations / Spruce Line.
74	
75	Two approved claims on file appear to contradict each other. The team is mid-conversation, so surface the conflict as one warm, natural, chat-shaped question that helps resolve it. Match the tone of a colleague who quietly noticed something off.
76	
77	Hard rules:
78	- Return ONLY the question text — no preface, no explanation, no metadata.
79	- 240 characters or less.
80	- One question only.
81	- Plain text, no markdown.
82	- If the suggested question is already concrete, you can use it nearly verbatim.
83	- Do not name the model, the system, or "contradictions" — just ask which one is right today.`;
84	
85	// Similarity threshold — cosine distance below this may indicate related claims.
86	// pgvector cosine distance: 0 = identical, 2 = maximally different.
87	const SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.35;
88	
89	// How many similar claims to retrieve per comparison.
90	const TOP_K = 8;
91	
92	const CONTRADICTION_PROMPT_VERSION = 'contradiction-1.0.0';
93	
94	// ─── Module-singleton OracleAIClient (mirrors R6/R7/R8/R9 workers) ──────────
95	function buildOracleClient(): OracleAIClient {
96	  return new OracleAIClient({
97	    adapters: buildStandardAdapters(),
98	    fallbackOnError: true,
99	  });
100	}

102	// ─── LLM adjudication schema: is this a contradiction? ──────────────────────
103	const ContradictionCheckSchema = z.object({
104	  isContradiction: z
105	    .boolean()
106	    .describe('True if the two claims assert conflicting facts about the same operational reality.'),
107	  explanation: z
108	    .string()
109	    .describe('One-sentence explanation of why this is or is not a contradiction.'),
110	  severity: z
111	    .enum(['low', 'medium', 'high'])
112	    .describe(
113	      'If a contradiction: how serious the conflict is. low=minor nuance, medium=significant disagreement, high=directly conflicting facts that cannot both be true.',
114	    ),
115	  suggestedQuestion: z
116	    .string()
117	    .optional()
118	    .describe(
119	      'A question that would resolve this contradiction if asked in a conversation.',
120	    ),
121	});
122	type ContradictionCheck = z.infer<typeof ContradictionCheckSchema>;
123	
124	const CONTRADICTION_ADJUDICATION_SYSTEM = `You are an operational knowledge consistency checker for The Oracle.
125	
126	Your task: determine whether two operational claims about the same company (POP Creations / Spruce Line) are contradictory.
127	
128	A CONTRADICTION exists when:
129	- Both claims address the same process, system, or operational rule, AND
130	- They assert incompatible facts (both cannot simultaneously be true).
131	
132	Not a contradiction:
133	- One claim is a general rule and the other is a recognized exception to that rule.
134	- The claims describe different aspects of the same process.
135	- The claims apply to different time periods, departments, or conditions.
136	- One claim is a refinement or elaboration of the other.
137	
138	Be precise: "We usually do X" vs "Sometimes we do Y" is NOT a contradiction.
139	"We always send to China first" vs "China receives it only after US approval" IS a contradiction.
140	
141	Return only the structured JSON matching the schema.`;
142	
143	// ─── Payload schema ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
144	const ClaimCheckPayloadSchema = z.object({
145	  claimId: z.string().uuid(),
146	});

148	// ─── Curated route resolver (mirrors claim-extraction.ts) ───────────────────
149	async function resolveContradictionRoute(
150	  db: ReturnType<typeof getDirectDb>,
151	): Promise<OracleModelRoute> {
152	  const resolved = await resolveRouteFromSettings(db, 'extraction');
153	  if (resolved) return resolved;
154	  const fallback = getOracleRoute(FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID);
155	  if (!fallback) {
156	    throw new Error(
157	      `[contradiction-watcher] default_extraction_route unset/unresolvable, and fallback "${FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID}" also missing.`,
158	    );
159	  }
160	  console.warn(
161	    `[contradiction-watcher] default_extraction_route unset/unresolvable; using fallback "${FALLBACK_ROUTE_ID}".`,
162	  );
163	  return fallback;
164	}
